candidates.title: Balthazar's Keuken
candidates.blurb: One three-course menu that changes weekly — whatever the market gave. Thirty years of quiet consistency on a Jordaan workshop street.

candidates.title: Eetsalon Van Dobben
candidates.blurb: Fluorescent-lit kroket-and-pekelvlees counter since 1945: white jackets, marble bar, broodjes at midnight. Amsterdam in sandwich form.
Specialty coffee by day, natural wine and a tight Nordic-leaning menu by night — Oost's all-day living room.

candidates.title: In 't Aepjen
candidates.blurb: Jenever by candlelight inside one of the city's two surviving wooden houses (1519), once a sailors' inn that took monkeys as payment.

candidates.title: Café 't Smalle
candidates.blurb: A 1786 jenever-distillery tasting room with the Jordaan's most golden interior and a canal-side jetty terrace worth plotting for.

candidates.title: De Drie Fleschjes
candidates.blurb: Tasting house from 1650 behind the Nieuwe Kerk: a wall of oak casks leased by regulars' families for generations. Order a kopstootje.

candidates.title: Lot Sixty One
candidates.blurb: A roastery hatch on the Kinkerbuurt corner pulling some of Europe's most awarded espresso; beans roast in the basement window.

candidates.title: Café de Dokter
candidates.blurb: Four square metres of 1798 bar run by the same family for generations: smoked beef, old jenever, dust left on purpose.

candidates.title: Café Papeneiland
candidates.blurb: Corner brown café since 1642, Delft-blue tiles and the apple pie Bill Clinton detoured for. The Jordaan's fireplace.

candidates.title: Laura Dols
candidates.blurb: Vintage legend since 1979: two floors of 1950s ballgowns, sheepskins and sequins — costume designers' open secret.

candidates.title: Het Oud-Hollandsch Snoepwinkeltje
candidates.blurb: A Jordaan sweetshop of apothecary jars: drop, borstplaat and forgotten Dutch candies weighed out by the ons.

candidates.title: Electric Ladyland
candidates.blurb: The world's first museum of fluorescent art: a Jordaan basement of glowing minerals and psychedelic sculpture, toured by its obsessive keeper.

candidates.title: Ons' Lieve Heer op Solder
candidates.blurb: A complete Catholic church hidden in a 1663 canal-house attic — the city's second-oldest museum and its best-kept devotional secret.
candidates.why: Full church hidden in an attic

candidates.title: Museum Het Schip
candidates.blurb: Flagship of Amsterdam School expressionism: a workers' palace of waving brick, with the original 1920s post office intact.

candidates.title: De Hollandsche Manege
candidates.blurb: A Vienna-style riding school from 1882 hidden behind Vondelpark: take coffee on the balcony foyer while horses drill below.

candidates.title: Zuiderbad
candidates.blurb: Swim laps under the 1912 vaulted roof of a former indoor cycling school — the city's most beautiful municipal pool.

candidates.title: Marineterrein
candidates.blurb: A decommissioned navy yard turned open innovation quarter — locals swim off the pontoon in filtered harbour water at lunch.
candidates.why: City swimming on a navy yard

candidates.title: Pianola Museum
candidates.blurb: A Jordaan parlour of self-playing pianos and 30,000 music rolls; candlelit concerts where the instrument is the ghost performer.

candidates.title: W139
candidates.blurb: Artist-run since a 1979 squat and still handing its Warmoesstraat hall to makers with no market brief. Raw by design.

candidates.title: De Appel
candidates.blurb: The contemporary-art institute that trains the world's curators: performance, discourse and exhibitions in a Nieuw-West broedplaats.

candidates.title: Framer Framed
candidates.blurb: Politically engaged exhibition hall in a former Oost factory: art at the seam of decolonisation, migration and the archive.

candidates.title: Oude Kerk
candidates.blurb: The city's oldest building doubles as its boldest contemporary-art hall: commissioned installations under a medieval wooden vault.
candidates.why: New art in the oldest building

candidates.title: Broek in Waterland
candidates.blurb: Cycle twenty minutes past the IJ ferry into a 17th-century painted-wood village; canoe the same ditches farmers skate in winter.

candidates.title: Naarden-Vesting
candidates.blurb: A perfect star-fort town of double moats, casemates and cheese warehouses — twenty-five minutes by train, centuries away.

candidates.title: Ouderkerk aan de Amstel
candidates.blurb: The classic Sunday ride: follow the Amstel past Rembrandt's sketching spot and the Riekermolen windmill to terraces at the old village.
candidates.why: The Amstel ride locals swear by

candidates.title: Texel
candidates.blurb: The Wadden island Amsterdammers actually take: dunes, lamb, lighthouse beaches and dark-sky nights, two hours door to ferry.

candidates.title: Nieuwmarkt Boerenmarkt
candidates.blurb: Small Saturday organic market in the shadow of the Waag: sourdough, raw-milk cheese and flowers without the Albert Cuyp crowds.

candidates.title: Zuidermrkt
candidates.blurb: Neighbourhood-run Saturday market: French cheeses, oysters and vinyl crates — Oud-Zuid at its most villagey.

candidates.title: Antiekcentrum Amsterdam
candidates.blurb: An indoor maze of seventy-plus antique stalls off the Elandsgracht: Delftware to Bakelite. The Jordaan's collective attic.
